title: anthyllis vulneraria f. calcicola
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria f. calcicola, also known as Kidney Vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the legume family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to support kidney function, alleviate symptoms of arthritis and rheumatism, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Anthyllis vulneraria f. calcicola can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support urinary health and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections.
